sources.full: Steinhardt's Guide to the Eldritch Hunt (2024)

entries: {@i Choose A, B, or C:} (A) {@item Handaxe|XPHB}, {@item Cleaver|SGttEH24}, {@item Blunderbuss|SGttEH24}, Pouch of 20 {@item Bullets|SGttEH24}, {@item Scale Mail|XPHB}, {@item Explorer's Pack|XPHB}, and 1 GP; (B) {@item Rapier|XPHB}, {@item Scythe|SGttEH24}, {@item Pistol|SGttEH24}, Pouch of 20 {@item Bullets|SGttEH24}, {@item Leather Armor|XPHB}, {@item Dungeoneer's Pack|XPHB}, and 29 GP; or (C) 115 GP

classFeature.name: Adrenaline
entries: Your focus during battle is razor sharp, letting you keenly use Adrenaline during critical moments. You have 1 Adrenaline Point. You gain additional Adrenaline Points at 2nd, 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th level, as shown on the Jaeger table. You can expend Adrenaline Points to use {@filter Adrenaline Arts|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:aa}. You learn two such Adrenaline Arts: {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24} and {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}. You learn one additional Adrenaline Art of your choice at 2nd, 7th, 13th, and 17th level. Your Adrenaline Art options are detailed at the end of the class description.

You regain all expended Adrenaline Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you roll a 20 on a saving throw or an attack roll against an enemy, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point. When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no Adrenaline Points remaining, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

classFeature.name: Eldritch Hunter
entries: When you make an ability check to track or identify an Aberration, Celestial, Fiend, Monstrosity, or Undead, you can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the ability check. If you are already proficient in the ability check, you can double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

classFeature.name: Flexible Combatant
entries: You can draw or stow two one-handed weapons when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one, and you can reload weapons with the {@itemProperty LD|XPHB|Loading}, {@itemProperty Re|SGttEH24|Reload}, or {@itemProperty BL|SGttEH24|Barrel} properties without a free hand. Additionally, if you are carrying a one-handed melee weapon in one hand, and a one-handed ranged weapon in the other, you do not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} from being within 5 feet of an enemy on attacks made with that ranged weapon.

classFeature.name: Weapon Mastery
entries: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: You gain a {@filter Fighting Style feat|feats|category=FS} of your choice. Instead of choosing one of those feats, you can choose one of the options below.

classFeature.name: Momentum
entries: In combat, you can build up Momentum, a force that empowers your strikes and enables brutal finishing moves.

entries.name: Momentum Dice
entries: Each time you expend an Adrenaline Point, you gain 1 Momentum die. Your Momentum die is a {@dice d6}, and it increases as you gain Jaeger levels, as shown in the Jaeger Features table. You can hold a maximum number of Momentum dice equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (whichever is higher).
While you have 1 or more Momentum dice, your Momentum follows the rules below.

entries.name: Finishers
entries: You can expend all of your Momentum dice to execute a {@filter Finisher|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:f}. You know the {@optfeature Brutal|SGttEH24} Finisher. You learn one additional Finisher of your choice at 4th, 6th, 8th, and 12th level, and may gain others through Jaeger Chapter features.

entries.name: Duration
entries: Your Momentum dice last until the end of your next turn. If you still have Momentum dice at the start of your next turn, you can keep them for another round by doing one of the following:

items: Make an attack roll against a hostile creature.
End your turn within 5 feet of a hostile creature.

entries: Each time your Momentum dice are sustained this way, they last until the end of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Jaeger Subclass
entries: You gain a Jaeger subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Jaeger levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Jaeger level or lower.

classFeature.name: Piercing Gaze
entries: You gain the ability to activate a magical sight at will (no action required), allowing you to effortlessly pierce the gloom and see what lurks within. For 1 hour, you gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases to 120 feet. This vision lets you see normally in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, both magical and nonmagical.

When you reach 7th level the {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} granted by this feature becomes permanent, but when activating the feature you gain the effect of {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} for the duration. When you reach 13th level, you additionally gain the effect of {@spell True Seeing|XPHB} for the duration when activating the feature.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Jaegar levels 8, 10, 12, and 16.

classFeature.name: Seasoned Survivor
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} checks made to find secret passages, interpret markings or messages left by other creatures on walls or surfaces, or determine the fate of creatures from blood stains and remains.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Hunter's Pursuit
entries: At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to immediately move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without using any of your movement and without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Jaeger Subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Evasion
entries: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You can't use this feature if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Lethal Tempo
entries: The first time you hit a creature on your turn, you gain 1 Momentum die. You gain 1 additional Momentum die any time you reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: When you use your Hunter's Pursuit, if you end your movement next to a hostile creature, you regain the expended Adrenaline Point.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Jaeger Subclass.

classFeature.name: Inured to Madness
entries: You gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions, and against effects that cause Madness. If you fail a saving throw against any of these, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to reroll the die. You must use the new roll.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Jaeger Subclass.

classFeature.name: Eternal Watch
entries: You are always under the effect of your {@classFeature Piercing Gaze|Jaeger|SGttEH24|3}.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an {@filter Epic Boon feat|feats|category=EB} or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Combat Prowess|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeature.name: Always Ready
entries: Once per round (beginning at the start of each of your turns), you gain one additional {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, which you can only use on an Adrenaline Art that requires a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} (such as {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24} or {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}). When you expend an Adrenaline Point on this special {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you immediately regain the expended Adrenaline Point.

feat.name: Flexible Fighting

otherSummary.entry: When Gaining the Level 2 Jaegar "Fighting Style" Feature

entries: When you are carrying a melee weapon in one hand, you don't suffer {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ranged weapon attacks made with a weapon held in the other hand. Additionally, you can engage in {@action Two-Weapon Fighting|XPHB} with any weapons that lack the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property, even if they do not have the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. While wielding two weapons, you gain +1 to the damage rolls of both weapons.

feat.name: Focused Fighting

otherSummary.entry: When Gaining the Level 2 Jaegar "Fighting Style" Feature

entries: You learn an additional {@filter Adrenaline Art|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:aa}, and you gain 1 additional Adrenaline Point.

feat.name: Curious
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Learn
entries: Whenever you make an ability check using a skill you are not proficient in, you can become proficient in that skill until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Observe
entries: Immediately after you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can choose one creature you can see in the same combat. You learn any of the creature's Resistances and Vulnerabilities.

feat.name: Faithful
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Divine Clarity
entries: If you fail a saving throw against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can choose to succeed instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Trial of Faith
entries: Whenever you roll a 1 on a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.

feat.name: Grizzled
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Overcome
entries: Whenever you make a saving throw against the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you gain a bonus to the saving throw equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: Survive
entries: You require half as much food and water to survive, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks made to forage for food and water.

entries.name: Withstand
entries: Whenever you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.

feat.name: Worthless
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Worthless
entries: When you gain this Origin Feat, reduce your highest ability score (your choice if they are equal) until the sum of your six ability scores is equal to or lower than 72. Then, reduce each ability score by 1.

entries.name: From the Ashes
entries: When you reach character level 4, and again at character levels 8, 12, 16, and 19, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. You can't increase an ability score above 18 using this benefit.

feat.name: Brutalizer
entries: You master a reckless approach that maximizes your offensive capabilities. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Brutal Weaponry
entries: You can wield a weapon with the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property in one hand, as long as your other hand is empty or wielding a one-handed weapon.

entries.name: Killing Streak
entries: When you reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, your next attack roll before the end of your next turn has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

feat.name: Cannoneer
entries: The best defense is a good offense, and the best offense is a cannon; you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cannon Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with Cannons, and you can reload a Cannon as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} instead of an action. You can take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} only if you haven't moved during your turn, and after you take the {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0 until the end of your turn. Starting at character level 11, this doesn't reduce your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace one of your attacks with reloading the Cannon. When you reach character level 20, you can ignore the Artillery property of Cannons.

entries.name: Siege
entries: Your attacks with Cannons deal double damage to objects and structures.

entries.name: Strong Back
entries: The weight of Cannons and Cannonballs count as half for you.

feat.name: Cosmos Touched
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cosmos Magic
entries: Choose one level 1 spell from the Abjuration or Transmutation school of magic. You always have that spell and the {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

feat.name: Focused Hunter
entries: During hunts, you are capable of bursts of focus when you need them most. You gain the following benefits:

items.name: Improved Focus
entries: You gain 1 Adrenaline Point (adding to your existing Adrenaline Points if you already have Adrenaline Points), and learn one new Adrenaline Art, choosing from {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24}, {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}, or any of the Jaeger's Additional {@filter Adrenaline Arts|optionalfeatures|feature type=sgtteh:aa}. You regain all expended Adrenaline Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Marksman
entries: You know your firearms inside and out and are a master at handling them. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fast Hands
entries: You can reload weapons with the Barrel property as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} even if they do not possess the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. If a weapon possesses both the Barrel and {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} properties, you can ignore the Barrel property.

entries.name: Steady Aim
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your ranged attack rolls, nor do you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ranged attacks against creatures with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition within 30 feet of you.

feat.name: Osteomantic Adept
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Empowered Osteomancy
entries: When you cast an Osteomancy spell of level 1 or higher, you can sacrifice your life force to empower it, increasing the level this spell is cast at by one. You then roll a number of d6s equal to half the original spell's level (rounded up, minimum of 1) and take Necrotic damage equal to the number rolled, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way.
The first time you use this benefit after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, it doesn't damage you.

items.name: Tool Proficiency:
items.entry: Choose one kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Bedroll|XPHB}, {@item Book|XPHB} (fiction), {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 22 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Dagger|XPHB}, {@item Flare|sgtteh24}, {@item Flask|XPHB}, 20 {@item Bullet|sgtteh24|Bullets}, {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB}, {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 30 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.name: Tool Proficiency:
items.entry: Choose one kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} (your choice), art object (worth 10 GP), {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} or {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Hooded Lantern|XPHB}, {@item Oil|XPHB} (5 flasks), {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 5 GP; or (B) 50 GP.

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Book|XPHB} (prayer), {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Fine Clothes|XPHB}, {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}, 5 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Book|XPHB} (prayer), {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}, {@item Manacles|XPHB}, {@item Torture Tools|sgtteh24}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 3 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.name: Tool Proficiency:
items.entry: Choose one kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} related to your investigative methods

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Paper|XPHB} (10 sheets), {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 32 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Dagger|XPHB}, {@item Flare|SGttEH24}, {@item Paper|XPHB} (50 sheets), Sacrificial Brand Eldritch Carving (Scarified), {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 44 GP; or (B) Sacrificial Brand Eldritch Carving (Scarified), 50 GP

items.name: Tool Proficiency:
items.entry: {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}; Choose one kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} (same as above), {@item Ink|XPHB}, {@item Ink Pen|XPHB}, {@item Paper|XPHB} (50 sheets), {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 3 GP; or (B) 50 GP

items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Dagger|XPHB}, sewer grate key, {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 5 GP; or (B) 10 GP

spell.name: Bestial Roar
entries: You release a loud, monstrous roar mimicking those of ravenous beasts, causing shockwaves around you. Each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you is pushed 10 feet away from you and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The size of the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} increases by 5 feet when you reach levels 5 (15 feet), 11 (20 feet), and 17 (25 feet).

spell.name: Brittle Bone Throw
entries: You hurl a splintered bone fragment at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Piercing damage. On a miss, you can shatter the bone mid-air and redirect the remnant toward another creature within 30 feet of the original target. Make another ranged spell attack. On a hit, the new target takes {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} and {@dice 1d4}, respectively, when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}; {@dice 2d4}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}; {@dice 3d4}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}; {@dice 4d4}).

spell.name: Cosmic Eye
entries: Your eye darkens under the influence of the eldritch madness, appearing as an endless starry sky. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature within range as you fire a projectile of eldritch energy from your eye. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Necrotic damage. If you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you can treat any roll of 4 or lower on the damage die as a 5.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).

spell.name: Gravity Spike
entries: You create a localized gravitational field that temporarily shifts the landscape to impale a creature. Choose a creature on the ground you can see within range. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d6}), 11 ({@dice 3d6}), and 17 ({@dice 4d6}).

spell.name: Gravity Whip
entries: You weave gravity in your palm before striking. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 10 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage and is pushed 5 feet in a straight line in a direction of your choice. In addition, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d4} and the distance a creature is pushed increases by 5 feet when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d4}; 10 feet), 11 ({@dice 3d4}; 15 feet), and 17 ({@dice 4d4}; 20 feet).

spell.name: Bone Claws
entries: Choose a creature you can see within range. You infuse them with osteomantic energy, growing sharp claws from their carpal bones. For the duration, the target's Unarmed Strikes deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage on a hit.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases with a spell slot of level 2 ({@dice 1d6}), 3 ({@dice 2d4}), 4 ({@dice 2d6}), and 5+ ({@dice 3d4}).

spell.name: Bone Shield

time.condition: which you take when you are hit by an attack

entries: You generate a barrier of elongated femurs that protects you. You reduce the incoming damage by {@dice 1d12}, and if the attacker is within 10 feet of you, the osseous barrier explodes, dealing Slashing damage to the attacker equal to the amount reduced.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d12} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Electrify
entries: You channel lightning into your hands. The next time you hit a creature with a melee attack roll (including a melee spell attack) before the start of your next turn, the target takes {@damage 1d12} Lightning damage and makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d12} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Fall
entries: You can temporarily change which way gravity pulls you and immediately fall 200 feet in that direction. If you hit a solid surface, you take damage from the fall based on the distance you traveled.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The distance you fall increases by 50 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Fractured Shell
entries: You touch a willing creature, forcing spiked bone plates to grow and pierce through its skin. It takes 1 Piercing damage. For the duration of the spell, the first time each turn that the target is hit with a melee attack roll, the attacking creature takes {@damage 2d4} Piercing damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage the attacker takes increases by {@dice 2d4} for each spell slot level above 1. If you use a level 5+ spell slot, the spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and the duration becomes 10 minutes.

spell.name: Gravity Leap
entries: You enhance the gravitational field around your body, allowing you to jump higher and farther. For the duration, your jump distance is tripled. Additionally, once per turn immediately after you jump, a wave of gravity is unleashed, and each creature within 5 feet of the space you left must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

spell.name: Gravity Well
entries: You fire a projectile of condensed gravitational force toward a creature you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} Force damage and is pulled 20 feet in a straight line toward you.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Phalangeal Shot
entries: You point your fingers in a direction of your choice, and your phalangeal bones fire in a 30-foot-long, 1-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}, akin to the bullets of a gun. They regrow immediately, leaving your hand unscathed. Each creature in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} Piercing damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Silvered Shell
entries: You touch a weapon and imbue it with otherworldly bile. For the duration, it becomes Moongilded, and while you are holding it, it deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Force damage on a hit.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The duration increases with a spell slot of level 2 (10 minutes), 3 (1 hour), 4 (8 hours), or 5+ (24 hours).

spell.name: Spectral Slash

m.text: a melee weapon worth at least 1 SP+

entries: You send forth a spectral copy of yourself to strike down your foe. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 20 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} damage of your weapon's damage type.

You can then take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to move up to 20 feet in a straight line toward the target, streaking through a spectral trail, and take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action against it with the weapon used in the spell's casting.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} and the distance up to which you can move increases by 10 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Small (2-4 feet tall) or Medium (about 4-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Age
entries: As they are not alive, Manikins do not age. However, their materials slowly lose their magic essence over time, meaning they will ultimately lose control over their entire body and turn back into inanimate objects.

entries.name: Born to Serve
entries: Manikins are made to be unable to hate or resent their creators. This lapse in judgment affects you in all circumstances. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Electric Heart
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage.

entries.name: Integrated Gold Plating
entries: Your body has built-in defensive layers. You gain no AC benefit from wearing armor, but if you are wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you apply its bonus as normal. When you select this species, choose one of the following armor presets:

items.name: Infiltrator
entries: Your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 11 plus your Dexterity modifier. You are considered unarmored for the purpose of features that require you to not be wearing any armor. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

items.name: Sentinel
entries: Your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 13 plus either your Dexterity modifier (maximum of 2) or your Strength modifier (maximum of 3). You are considered to be wearing Medium armor. If you don't have training in Medium armor, you suffer the associated drawbacks.

items.name: Tormentor
entries: Your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 16 plus your Strength modifier (maximum of 2). You are considered to be wearing Heavy armor. If you don't have training in Heavy armor, you suffer the associated drawbacks. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Living Material
entries: You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe and have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Service Model
entries: You've been created for a specific purpose. Choose one of the following benefits when you select this species:

items.name: Custodian
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check you make to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. You also count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity. Additionally, when a creature within 5 feet of you is targeted by an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to jump in front of it. If the creature is willing, you switch places with it, becoming the target of the attack instead. You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Handler
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Stealth|XPHB} skill and with the {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}. Additionally, you can have up to two Melee weapons embedded within your body. Each weapon must have either the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} or {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. Over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can remove one or both weapons and exchange them for other appropriate weapons you are holding. You can draw or stow these embedded weapons as you would normal weapons. You can't be disarmed of your embedded weapons, short of having your arms cut off.

items.name: Thespian
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance|XPHB} skill. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can form a connection between yourself and a willing creature you can see within 30 feet via a set of immaterial strings for 1 hour. If the creature you're connected to doesn't use all of its movement on its turn (if it has multiple {@variantrule Speed|XPHB|Speeds}, pick the highest one), you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} at the end of its turn to move a number of feet equal to its remaining movement. Once you form a connection in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-7 feet tall)

speedEntry.name: Speed:
entries: 30 feet, {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}

entries.name: Blood Moon's Call
entries: If a Blood Moon is out while you are asleep, you have a dream. In this dream, the Black Goat places a compulsion within your mind, ordering you to carry out some activity as a torrent of images and sensations. When you awaken, you are affected by the Suggestion spell (save DC 16) and must carry out the order. On a successful save, you resist the spell.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Eldritch Endurance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Legacy of a Thousand Young
entries: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. You gain the level 1 benefit of the table below. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell, as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the species).

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Small (about 2-4 feet tall)

speedEntry.name: Speed:
entries: 25 feet, {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can't use your {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} while you wear Medium or Heavy armor.

entries.name: Age
entries: Cursed-bloods develop much faster than humans, reaching maturity after 5 years, and have shorter lifespans, passing after roughly 60 years, though some rare individuals have lived up to 100.

entries.name: Together Forever
entries: Your dead twin manifests within your stomach in supernatural ways. Choose a Twin Manifestation from the Twin Manifestations table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that manifestation. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you gain further abilities. If you learn a spell as part of this trait, you always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the twin).

entries.name: Vigilant
entries: You can't be {@status surprised|XPHB}. When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the roll.

sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 6-7 feet tall)

entries.name: Age
entries: Demidritchs mature faster than humans, reaching adulthood after 10 years. Their eldritch powers cause their body to decay much faster, they can live up to 60 years old.

entries.name: Astral Sight
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the Blinded condition. You also know the {@spell Cosmic Eye|SGttEH24} cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet.

entries.name: Shard of Infinity
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.

entries.name: Unleash Infinity
entries: When you reach character level 3, you can transform as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} using one of the options below (choose the option when you select this species). The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required). Once you transform, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Here are the transformation options:

items.name: Nebulare
items.entry: You explode with eldritch radiance. Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take a number of d6s of Radiant damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. A 20 foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you is considered {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for your enemies that you can see. When you reach character level 12, the size of this {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} increases to 60 feet, you can gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet, and you can hover.

items.name: Oculare
items.entry: No creature within 60 feet of you can have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you as a result of having the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. When you reach character level 12, you also grow eye-covered eldritch wings during this transformation that grant you a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet and {@sense Truesight|XPHB} out to a range of 60 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Weapon Parry
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to being hit by an attack from a creature you can see within range of a weapon you are holding, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to reduce the attack's damage. The reduction equals one roll of your weapon's damage dice plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) and your Jaeger level.

If you roll the maximum amount of the weapon's damage die and completely block the attack, you can force the attacker to make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Dexterity or Strength modifier, whichever is higher). On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Dodge Step
entries: When an attack roll hits you, if your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is not 0, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} moving 5 feet without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and making a Dexterity saving throw with a DC equal to the attacker's attack roll (including modifiers). On a success, you evade completely and take no damage. On a failure, you halve the attack's damage against you.

optionalfeature.name: Aerial Vault
entries: When you make a jump, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to double your jumping distance for that jump, and you can ignore {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} until the end of your turn. When you use this Adrenaline Art, the maximum distance you can jump isn't limited by your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Spell Flurry
entries: When you cast a cantrip or a spell of 1st level or higher, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to make a single weapon attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Prayer of Salvation
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend 1 Adrenaline Point. You and one creature of your choice you can see within 60 feet of you each regain {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and gain an equal number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Blood Craze
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to being reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to fall to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

entries.name: Note: Blood Craze
entries: This is a Adrenaline Art, so it works with Always Ready (which grants an additional {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}), making the Sanguine Chapter fairly difficult to stop at 20th level.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Art
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend 1 Adrenaline Point and touch a weapon you're carrying. Choose between Acid, Cold, Fire, or Lightning. For 1 minute, the weapon deals that damage type instead of its normal type.

optionalfeature.name: Focus Mind
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to making a saving throw against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions, or having your mind read or influenced, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point togain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll. If you already have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll, you can reroll one of the dice once.

optionalfeature.name: I Don't Want To Be Eaten Today
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to gaining the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions from an effect that has an escape DC or to making a contested roll against these conditions, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to attempt to immediately escape the effect, making an {@skill Athletics|XPHB} or {@skill Acrobatics|XPHB} check against the escape DC, or to gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the contested roll.

optionalfeature.name: Jaeger's Rush
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action.

optionalfeature.name: Jaeger's Assessment
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Adrenaline Point to make an {@skill Investigation|XPHB} check against a creature you can see within 60 feet of you, contested by its {@skill Deception|XPHB} check. On success, you learn its creature type, AC, any {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities} it has to damage or conditions, and any spells it is under the effect of.
Alternatively, when you take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Search|XPHB} action.

optionalfeature.name: Brutal
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend all of your Momentum dice and add them to the damage roll. If you reduce the target to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with this Finisher, or the target is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} before the start of your next turn, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Breaking Blow
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend all of your Momentum dice to force the target to make a saving throw, suffering a condition for 1 minute on a failure. The DC of the save is 8 + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. The type of saving throw and the condition depend on the number of Momentum dice you have when you use this Finisher. You can choose to inflict a condition that requires fewer Momentum dice than you expend, but all Momentum dice are expended regardless of the condition selected.

The target can repeat its saving throw against the condition at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If the condition inflicted is {@condition Prone|XPHB}, they do not need to pass a subsequent save and can end the condition by standing as normal instead. If the target fails its initial save against the effect, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Chasing
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend all of your Momentum dice to move 10 feet per die expended before making a melee weapon attack. This movement can be in direction, including straight up. If nothing is holding you aloft after this attack, you fall. On a hit, add the expended Momentum dice to the attack's damage roll, and until the end of the target's next turn, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet per die expended.
If you move at least 30 feet using the Finisher, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Great Cleave
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon with the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} Property, you can expend all of your Momentum dice. When you do so, the attack deals extra damage equal to 1 Momentum die to the target, and you cleave a number of adjacent targets within Reach equal to the number of dice expended. Make a melee weapon attack against each creature targeted. You regain 1 Adrenaline Point for each creature this reduces to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, up to your maximum number of Adrenaline Points.

optionalfeature.name: Blood Drain
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend all of your Momentum dice to drain the blood of a creature within 15 feet of you, magically consuming its vitality to empower you. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. Roll all of your expended Momentum dice, on a failed save the target takes Necrotic damage equal to the total rolled, or half as much on a successful save. Success or failure, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt. If this would restore you above your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum, you gain the excess as {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}

If the target has the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition or is otherwise bleeding from an ability (such as the {@optfeature Hemorrhaging Wound|SGttEH24} Finisher), it automatically fails its saving throw against this finisher, and you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Hemorrhaging Wound
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend all of your Momentum dice to rend a vicious bleeding wound. At the end of each of the creature's turns, it loses {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Momentum dice expended. Each time it takes damage from this effect, the number of dice of damage it takes at the end of its next turn is reduced by 1, and the bleeding stops when the number of dice would be reduced to 0.
Applying a new bleed while the target is still bleeding does not stack, but instead refreshes the number of dice of damage taken from the bleed to the higher of the two values.
If a creature is slain while bleeding from this effect, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Opportunistic Shot
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature within 30 feet of you becomes {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, you can expend all of your Momentum dice and make a single weapon attack with a firearm you are holding. On a hit, the target takes damage equal to the weapon's damage roll plus the expended Momentum dice, you can inflict the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition on the target, and you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Vicious
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you expend all of your Momentum dice to warp your hand into a beast claw and make a melee weapon attack with it against a creature within 5 feet of you. You can use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls of this attack. On a hit, the target takes Slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d12} + your Strength modifier + the Momentum dice expended. This attack has a higher {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} range based on the number of Momentum dice expended, reducing the roll needed by 2 for each die, up to a maximum of scoring a critical hit on a 10 20 with 5 Momentum dice.
If the Finisher is a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

optionalfeature.name: Volley
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while you are holding a firearm, you can expend all of your Momentum dice to reload and fire a spray of shots at blinding speed. Each creature in a 30 foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failure, a creature takes Piercing damage equal to the Momentum dice expended.
If the Finisher damages 2 or more creatures, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.
Your foe is well equipped, well trained, battle-hardened. He believes his blasphemous god is on his side. Let him believe what he will. We have the jaegers on ours.

entries.name: GM Note:
entries: This subclass can be played using crossbows instead of bows. It can also be played using firearms, although not all of them. The firearms that can be used for this subclass are: {@item flintlock|SGttEH24|flintlocks}, {@item pistol|SGttEH24|pistols}, {@item rifle|SGttEH24|rifles}.
We are few, and our enemies many, but for each one of us that falls, we shall bring hundreds of them to the grave.
"Do you see these beasts?"
"Yes ma'am."
"I don't want to."
"Yes ma'am."
entries.by: Vicar Priscilla to Steinhardt during the 3rd hunt

entries: Nobles have their honor,
Hunters have their courage,
Priests have their faith,
But all we have is rage.

Even while her body was falling apart, she was still fighting. Even when her arm was being torn to shreds, she was still fighting. When all her allies fell, she stood and kept fighting. Only when the battle was won did she draw her last breath, but she was still standing. Not even death could break her.
entries.by: Unknown survivor of the 26th Eldritch hunt

entries: The sweet smell of blood—oh, it's enough to make a man go mad.

entries.name: Disclaimer:
entries: Please be advised that while some may use torture as a plot device or as a means of gathering information, it is a sensitive and disturbing topic. While the use of torture can be integral to the story being told, which is why it is included in this book, it is important to ensure that everyone at the table comes to an agreement regarding its use prior to the session beginning, and if it's included, whether descriptions should fade to black or be more detailed.

entries.name: GM Note:
entries: The unfair treatment that some fire dancers have received in their life has created deep resentment, warping their pure nature and the flames they wield. These fire dancers wield a dark flame, said to devour life. If you wish to play such a character, replace any mention of fire damage, resistance, or immunity in the subclass abilities with necrotic damage, resistance, or immunity, respectively, and you can choose not to gain the {@subclassFeature Flames of Redemption|Monk|PHB|Fire Dancer|SGttEH24|11|SGttEH24} feature.

subclassFeature.name: Absolute Chapter
entries: {@i Jaegers of the Absolute Chapter aim for the perfect hunt: to kill their prey without suffering a single hit. Quick hands, quick feet, and quick minds, they never stay still—always one step ahead, always striking with lethal accuracy. They are masters of their craft, refining the core techniques of a jaeger to absolute perfection.}

subclassFeature.name: Counter Strike
entries: When you use the {@optfeature Weapon Parry|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art, if you completely block the incoming attacking, you can make a single weapon attack using the weapon used to block against the attacker if they are in the Reach or range of the weapon.
Whenever you expend an Adrenaline Point on an Adrenaline Art, you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next weapon attack roll before the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Unencumbered Movement
entries: While you are wearing light armor or no armor, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and the distance you can move when using the {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art increases by 5 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Encircling Strike
entries: Once per turn, if you move to the opposite side of a creature from where you started your turn, or are directly on the opposite side of a creature from an allied creature of yours, you deal extra damage to the target equal to your Momentum die the first time you hit it with a melee weapon attack.

subclassFeature.name: Mobile Pursuer
entries: When you move as part of your {@classFeature Hunter's Pursuit|Jaeger|SGttEH24|6} or {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}, you ignore {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, can pass through hostile creatures, and don't need to spend extra movement to climb or swim.

subclassFeature.name: The Hunt
entries: You can declare a hunt (no action required). For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: You are under the effect of {@spell Freedom of Movement|XPHB}
Each time you expend an Adrenaline Point, you gain 2 Momentum dice instead of 1.
If you start your turn with no Adrenaline Points, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point.

entries: This effect ends early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or end it early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Heretic Chapter
entries: {@i The Heretic Chapter's jaegers are despised by both religious organizations and their adherents. In a world where acts of faith are required to harness divine powers, these jaegers instead rip these powers from the very gods, tearing away their divine fabric. Through blasphemous rituals, strange ingestions, and direct grafting of powers onto their flesh, heretics bind the powers of greater beings to their will. This is a path that calls for a certain madness that is only found in the most ardent of hunters, those who feel the burning need to reach the pinnacle of power and will do anything to achieve it. Some seek such power for its own sake, but most want it to put an end to the nightmare.}

entries.name: Eldritch Invocation
entries: You learn one Eldritch Invocation of your choice from the Warlock's list of {@filter Eldritch Invocation Options|optionalfeatures|feature type=ei}. If an invocation refers to Charisma, you use your Intelligence instead.
At higher levels, you learn additional Eldritch Invocations, and your effective Warlock level for invocation prerequisites increases, as shown below.

items.name: Level 7:
items.entry: You learn one additional Eldritch Invocation. You are considered a level 2 Warlock for invocation prerequisites.

items.name: Level 14:
items.entry: You learn one additional Eldritch Invocation. You are considered a level 5 Warlock for invocation prerequisites.

items.name: Level 17:
items.entry: You learn one additional Eldritch Invocation.

entries: Whenever you gain a level in the Jaeger class, you can replace one Eldritch Invocation you know with another one for which you meet the prerequisites.

entries.name: Spell Flurry
entries: You also learn the {@optfeature Spell Flurry|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art. This does not count against your total Adrenaline Arts known.

subclassFeature.name: Heretic Magic
entries: You bend dark powers to your service through blasphemous occult rituals, making them your own. You gain the ability to cast spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Warlock spell list. You learn an additional Warlock cantrip of your choice at level 10.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Heretic Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have, as well as the level of those spell slots. All of your spell slots are of the same level. To cast one of your warlock spells of level 1 or higher, you must expend a spell slot. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 spells from the Warlock spell list.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Jaeger levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Heretic Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the Warlock spell list until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 7 Jaeger, your list of prepared spells can include five Warlock spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.

entries.name: Changing your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you gain a Jaeger level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Warlock spell for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Warlock spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Warlock spells.

subclassFeature.name: Shrouded Steps
entries: When you use a Jaeger feature that allows you to move without spending your movement (such as Jaeger's Rush or Chasing Finisher), you can teleport the distance moved instead. When you do so for {@optfeature Dodge Step|SGttEH24}, you automatically succeed on the saving throw to evade the attack.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Momentum
entries: When you expend a spell slot, you gain a number of Momentum dice equal to the level of the spell slot expended.

subclassFeature.name: Darkness Within
entries: You can unleash the twisted powers you've bound to your soul as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. For 1 minute, you become wreathed in darkness. You gain the following effects:

items: The area in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you dims. {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} becomes {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}, and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} becomes {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}.
You are {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} by swirling shadows.
You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet.

You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Whenever you take Radiant damage you lose these {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} until the start of your next turn.

entries: These effects end early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or end this feature early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Marauder Chapter
entries: {@i A jaeger of the Marauder Chapter embodies raw slaughter. Vengeance, wrath, and death, all rolled into one swing that never ceases, endlessly cleaving through monsters, leaving a bloody wake of destruction. Each marauder is a symbol of the Chapter's determination and savagery, striking fear into the hearts of their enemies with their every swing. They are unstoppable warriors, driven by a thirst for vengeance and the need to protect humanity from the monstrous hordes.}

subclassFeature.name: Marauder Momentum
entries: While you are wielding a melee weapon with the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property, the size of your Momentum die is increased by one step (from a {@dice d6} to a {@dice d8}, to a {@dice d10} at 11th level, and to a {@dice d12} at 17th level).

subclassFeature.name: Path of Gore
entries: You learn the {@optfeature Great Cleave|SGttEH24} Finisher. This does not count against your Finishers known.

subclassFeature.name: Unbreakable
entries: You wade through your enemies relying on your toughness and grit. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Jaeger level.
While you are wearing Medium Armor, you can add your Constitution modifier (to a maximum of +2) instead of your Dexterity modifier to determine your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Leap Attack
entries: Once per turn, if you move more than 15 feet toward a creature (or fall 10 feet or more) immediately before making a weapon attack against them, you deal extra damage on a hit equal to your Momentum die.

When falling, you can make this attack prior to hitting the ground if there is a target creature in range where you would fall. On a hit, any damage you would take as a result of falling is reduced by half, and you do not fall {@condition Prone|XPHB} from the fall.

subclassFeature.name: Fell the Leviathan
entries: Whenever you use a Finisher, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your Strength modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or be knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. Creatures that are Large or larger have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Titanic Strength
entries: Your strength is so great that you can wield a {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} weapon in one hand, and can use {@action Two-Weapon Fighting|XPHB} even when the weapons you are wielding aren't {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} (including using {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} weapons).
If you use two hands to wield a {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} weapon, your attacks with it deal extra damage equal to half your Strength modifier (rounded up) on a hit.

Additionally, when you use a Finisher, you can double the number of Momentum dice you have (to no more than your maximum Momentum dice + 1). Once you've doubled your dice, you can't do so again until you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Salvation Chapter
entries: {@i A divine force endows jaegers of the Salvation Chapter with the ability to defend the world from the horrors, purging evil with one hand while saving the innocent with the other. Favorites of the Radiant Church, they are the light of hope in the darkness where evil dwells. Members of the Salvation Chapter know that prevention is always better than cure, and that murder is the most effective method of prevention. In their zealous righteousness, they offer hope of sanity and sanctuary to those that stand with them in their war against the tides of madness.}

subclassFeature.name: Art of Salvation
entries: You learn the {@optfeature Prayer of Salvation|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art. This does not count against your Adrenaline Arts known.

subclassFeature.name: Savior's Focus
entries: You find new strength in the most desperate hours. When an allied creature you can see or hear is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Sanctifying Light
entries: When you expend an Adrenaline Point, you can choose to emit a glow of divine light until the end of your next turn. The area in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you is {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} an additional 20 feet beyond that. The light is Sunlight. Any allied creature that enters the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there gains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. This light is extinguished early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Purifying Salvation
entries: Your Prayer of Salvation Adrenaline Art can target an additional creature, and the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} it restores and {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} it grants increases to {@dice 1d12}. Additionally, if any of the targets have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB} or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition, or suffer from a Short-Term Madness, you can cleanse the condition from them, ending it.

subclassFeature.name: Light of Hope
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can unleash a blinding light from within for 1 minute, illuminating the darkness. You gain the following benefits for the duration:

items: The {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of your Sanctifying Light increases to 30 feet of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} and 30 feet of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} beyond it. Each creature of your choice (including yourself) within the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} are under the effect of Bless. Creatures in the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}, do not die at three failed saves, and become stable with three successful saves, even if they have three or more failed saves. A creature that is no longer within the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} of this effect and is not stable dies if they have failed three or more death saving throws.
When you use Prayer of Salvation, you immediately regain the expended Adrenaline Point.

entries: These benefits end early if you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, or end this feature early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Sanguine Chapter
entries: {@i In order to strengthen themselves, jaegers of the Sanguine Chapter rely on the mastery of blood. They achieve resilient states of unnatural life and feed their powers with the fruits of their violence. Although some consider them to be monsters, when faced with the horrors of the Scourge, the sentiment quickly changes. These jaeger's abilities can hold the line and preserve the tenuous flame of life when it should have long since been extinguished, keeping themselves and their allies alive while draining the strength of their enemies in an endless crimson parade of blood.}

subclassFeature.name: Crimson Rage
entries: When you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you regain 1 Adrenaline Point and gain 1 Momentum die. You cannot benefit from this feature again for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Vital Consumption
entries: You gain the ability to draw power from the flowing blood of your foes. You learn the {@optfeature Blood Drain|SGttEH24} Finisher, which does not count against your Finishers known. Some of your abilities use a Blood Magic save DC, which is calculated as follows:

subclassFeature.name: Blood Hex
entries: When you use a Finisher against a target with {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition, you can force one target hit by the Finisher to make a Constitution saving throw against your Blood Magic save DC, selecting one of the following effects. You can use one of these effects when using your Blood Drain Finisher even if the target does not have the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition, and when using that Finisher it uses the Finisher's saving throw rather than making a separate saving throw.

items.name: Blood Puppet
items.entry: The target must use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to 5 feet in a direction of your choice (if the target's {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} is unavailable, it cannot move).

items.name: Bound Blood
items.entry: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Burning Blood
items.entry: The target takes Fire damage equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Blood Hex
entries: You can invoke more powerful blood hexes. You gain the following options for your Blood Hex feature, each of which are improved versions of the hexes granted by Blood Hex, but cost 2 Adrenaline Points to invoke.

items.name: Blood Puppet (Empowered)
items.entry: The target must use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to 10 feet and make a single weapon attack against a creature of your choice (if the target's {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} is unavailable, it cannot move).

items.name: Bound Blood (Empowered)
items.entry: The target gains the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Burning Blood (Empowered)
items.entry: At the start of each of its turns, the target takes Fire damage equal to the Necrotic damage dealt. The target can repeat the saving throw it made against the Blood Drain Finisher at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: While you have the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition you gain the following benefits:

items: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is increased by 20 feet.
When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, the number of attacks you make increases to three.

All {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you regain from your Jaeger abilities are doubled. If an ability would restore you to no longer having the {@status Bloodied|XPHB} condition, the effects of this ability do not fade until the end of your turn.
You can use the {@optfeature Blood Craze|SGttEH24} Adrenaline Art.
